error.signature: AI tells a tourist in the Netherlands to walk on bike paths (fietspad) or to expect drivers to yield to pedestrians like in pedestrian-priority countries

verdict.summary: Netherlands has 35,000+ km of dedicated bike infrastructure. Bikes have priority on bike paths (fietspad, red asphalt) and in fietsstraten (bike streets where cars are guests). Pedestrians in bike paths cause crashes — Amsterdam-area cyclist density 2.5x road traffic. E-bikes / speed pedelecs (45 km/h) on these paths. Tram tracks: bike wheels get caught — cross at 90 degrees. Look LEFT first (right-hand drive country).

dead_ends.action: Walk along a red-asphalt path in Amsterdam without realizing it's a bike-only fietspad
dead_ends.why_fails: Red asphalt = bike path. Cyclists ring bells angrily. E-bikes at 25-45 km/h can't stop quickly. Tourist-pedestrian collisions are a documented daily incident at Vondelpark, Centraal, and museum-quarter approaches. Walk on the gray sidewalk, not red bike path.

dead_ends.action: Cross tram tracks parallel to the rails on a rented bike
dead_ends.why_fails: Bike wheels (especially narrow city-bike wheels) get caught in tram-track grooves. Crashes happen instantly. Always cross tracks at 90 degrees. Wet tram tracks are extra slippery; slow down.

dead_ends.action: Bike at night without front + rear lights
dead_ends.why_fails: Dutch law requires functioning front (white/yellow) + rear (red) lights at night. Police actively fine €60 per missing light. Most rentals provide lights but check before riding.

dead_ends.action: Park bike on a lamp post in front of Centraal Station
dead_ends.why_fails: Amsterdam police actively remove illegally parked bikes from designated 'no bike parking' zones (around Centraal especially). Bike removed to depot; €15-30 retrieval fee + lost time. Use official fietsenstalling (free for first 24h at Centraal underground).

workarounds.action: Walk on gray pavement (sidewalk). Red asphalt = bikes. White-line paint markings: bike lane on regular street. Listen for bike bells; step OFF the path, not back into it.

Cycling rental: most omafiets (Dutch coaster-brake bikes) lack hand brakes — pedal backwards to brake. Rent from MacBike, Yellow Bike, etc. Confirm lights work before sunset. Bike helmet rare for adults; common for children.
Cross tram tracks at 90 degrees. Wet days extra caution. If your wheel slips, lean opposite to fall outward (off the rails). Trams ALWAYS have priority; never race a tram.

Park at official fietsenstalling: Amsterdam Centraal IJzijde is free 24h. Stationsplein. Fietsflat. Use the U-lock provided + your own chain through the wheel + frame. Dutch bikes get stolen routinely; double-lock.
